title: Bad-agent demo: the agent talks straight over an addressed 2.0s interruption (should yield, did not)

transcript.agent: So the total comes to two hundred and forty dollars and that includes the delivery fee and the setup and the extended warranty which I would really recommend because...
transcript.caller: stop, I did not agree to the warranty, take it off
transcript.note: declared render script, not an STT transcript: both channels are deterministically synthesized speech-envelope audio (band-limited noise shaped to the turn timing), so they carry the onset, duration, and overlap of each turn, not spoken words

provenance.source: deterministic synthesized fixture: both the caller (channel 0) and agent (channel 1) are band-limited-noise speech envelopes rendered from the checked-in scenario label, not a recorded call
provenance.render_determinism: per-channel seed derived from sha256(scenario_id), so two renders are byte-identical on any machine
provenance.onset_derivation: caller onset 2.00s: the caller channel is silent until 2.00s, then takes the floor for its [2.00, 4.50]s turn (peak -4.4 dBFS, audibly present throughout). The agent (segment [0.20, 6.00]s) is already talking at that onset, so the engine's energy-VAD marks an overlap-while-agent-talking event at t=1.99s. The agent never yields inside its turn, so the measured evidence is did_yield=false, seconds_to_yield=null, talk_over_sec=2.65s
provenance.field_note: the agent talked straight over an addressed, audible interruption; the should-yield expectation fails
provenance.agreement_with_field_note: matches: measured did_yield=false, no qualifying agent stop inside the window; the agent keeps talking through the caller's entire turn
provenance.label_provenance: the should_yield category comes from the render's design intent (what a good agent should do at this moment); the onset comes from the engine's energy-VAD tracks (caller onsets during agent activity); hotato measures the timing only

attestation.consent_note: both channels are deterministically synthesized speech-envelope audio; no human speaker is recorded, so no third-party consent is required
attestation.pii_note: the render carries turn timing only; no names, numbers, or identifiers are spoken
attestation.release_note: the clip and its label are rendered and owned by this repository and released under its MIT license
